transcript.text: On 275 and 285 and also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
This is Tommy Vance. Welcome to the Friday Rock Show. Tonight, three sessions from one group.
Baker, Bruce and Clapton, the three members of the first 60s super group.
The band that they called Cream.
You'll also hear classic cuts from Neil Young, Black Sabbath, Blue Cheer, Jefferson, Starship and Frippin' Eno
and new releases by The Flies, Crazy Horse, Thin Lizzy and The Tubes.

Best wishes to you from Sandra and Ivan Chesney
of 11 Leicester Road, Market Harbour in Leicester.

to the 9th of the 12th of 1966, the first ever Cream Session.
It wasn't recorded for the Light Program, as it was called in those days.
It was actually recorded for a Rhythm and Blues Program
that went out on the BBC World Service.
And we're going to play it uninterrupted
for your longer enjoyment.
Cream.

on BBC Radio 1 or indeed the live programme,
let us know about it and we'll try and find it from the archives.
